description_long: This is a traditional pitchfork with a long, straight shaft and three evenly spaced, pointed tines at the top. The tines are metallic and have a slightly dark, weathered finish, giving them a robust and utilitarian appearance. The shaft appears to be made of metal as well, matching the tines in color and texture, and tapers to a pointed end at the bottom. The transition between the handle and the tines is smooth, with no visible grip or cap at the end of the handle. The overall design is simple and functional, suitable for tasks such as lifting or moving loose material like hay or debris. The object has a utilitarian, industrial look, making it appropriate for use in urban maintenance or gardening contexts.
description: A metal pitchfork with three pointed tines and a long, straight handle, featuring a dark, weathered finish. The design is simple and utilitarian, suitable for urban maintenance or gardening.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing all three pointed tines and the full length of the straight handle.
description_view_1: Side view showing the thin profile of the pitchfork, with the tines aligned and the handle appearing as a narrow line.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually similar to the front, displaying the three tines and the straight handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin profile with the tines and handle aligned.
